cuisine: Western / Global Health Food — Pantry staple widely available in Singapore
region_of_origin: United States (peanuts originating in South America; modern peanut butter commercialised in USA, 1895)

ontology.foodex2_description: Natural peanut butter (no added salt or sugar)
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for natural peanut butter as a food product; SNOMED CT coverage of specific food preparations is limited.

allergens: peanuts (tree-nut adjacent — Arachis hypogaea; most common severe food allergy in Singapore)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of natural peanut butter is approximately 2 tablespoons (32 g), providing roughly 192 kcal, 8 g protein, 16 g fat, and 6.4 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Peanut Butter Natural — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #16098
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy #16098 is a direct match for natural peanut butter (no added salt or sugar). All per-100g nutrient values are sourced from this entry. Confidence: HIGH.

gi_source.title: Peanut butter, GI value 14 — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (Glycemic Index Research Service)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 14 for peanut butter is a directly measured value from the University of Sydney GI Database, one of the lowest GI values recorded for any food. GL per 32 g serving = (14 × 6.4 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 0.9. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium ~8 mg per 100 g (essentially negligible); high potassium (558 mg) and magnesium (169 mg) support vasodilation and blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Natural peanut butter (no added salt) is one of the lowest-sodium calorie-dense foods available and is suitable for hypertensive patients; its high potassium-to-sodium ratio (~70:1) and magnesium content actively support blood pressure control, though portion size should be managed due to high energy density (600 kcal/100 g).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~14 (negligible glycaemic response); high protein (25 g) and fat (50 g) blunt postprandial glucose rise; oleic acid (MUFA ~24.5 g) improves insulin receptor sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Natural peanut butter has a GI of 14 and a glycaemic load of only 0.9 per standard 2-tablespoon (32 g) serving, making it an excellent snack choice for people with Type 2 Diabetes; the combination of protein, MUFA, and fibre produces strong satiety with minimal postprandial glucose excursion, and oleic acid has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High MUFA (oleic acid ~24.5 g/100 g) and PUFA (~12.3 g/100 g) with moderate saturated fat (10.5 g/100 g); zero trans fat; high vitamin E (9.1 mg) and folate (87 mcg) provide antioxidant and homocysteine-lowering benefits
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Natural peanut butter's favourable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio (~3.5:1), zero trans fat, and high vitamin E content support cardiovascular health; regular moderate consumption (1–2 tbsp/day) is associated with reduced LDL cholesterol and lower CVD risk in epidemiological studies, though total caloric intake must be monitored given its energy density.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Oleic acid (MUFA) anti-inflammatory properties; vitamin E antioxidant protection for joint tissue; folate relevant for methotrexate users
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Natural peanut butter may benefit rheumatoid arthritis patients through its oleic acid (anti-inflammatory MUFA), vitamin E (9.1 mg/100 g antioxidant), and folate (87 mcg/100 g, relevant for patients on methotrexate); however, peanut allergy must be confirmed absent before recommending, as peanut is among the most common severe food allergens in Singapore.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Natural peanut butter (no added sugar or salt) is a minimally processed spread made exclusively from ground roasted peanuts (Arachis hypogaea), with no hydrogenated oils, added sugar, or salt. Per 100 g it provides approximately 600 kcal, 25 g protein, 50 g fat (of which ~24.5 g is heart-healthy oleic acid MUFA, ~12.3 g PUFA, and 10.5 g saturated fat), 20 g carbohydrate, 6 g dietary fibre, and only ~8 mg sodium — essentially negligible. The glycaemic index is 14 (University of Sydney GI Database), one of the lowest values measured for any food, with a glycaemic load of only 0.9 per standard 2-tablespoon (32 g) serving. It is rich in magnesium (169 mg), potassium (558 mg), phosphorus (358 mg), vitamin E (9.1 mg), niacin (13.1 mg), and folate (87 mcg). Texture is creamy or chunky depending on grinding; flavour is rich, roasted, and slightly bitter with natural peanut oils. Widely available in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and specialty health stores. CRITICAL ALLERGEN: peanut is the most common cause of severe anaphylaxis in Singapore — always confirm absence of peanut allergy before recommending. Aflatoxin risk is managed by SFA regulation (<15 ppb). Source: USDA SR Legacy #16098. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy #16098 (exact match for natural peanut butter, no added salt or sugar), an approved primary source. GI value of 14 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (directly measured). Ontology codes (FoodOn FOODON_03316419, FoodEx2 A0PNB) are defensible direct matches. Confidence: HIGH.
notes: Cholesterol is 0.0 mg per 100 g: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #16098 — peanut butter is a plant-derived food containing no dietary cholesterol. Vitamin A is 0.0 mcg RAE: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #16098 — peanuts contain no preformed vitamin A. Vitamin C is 0.0 mg: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #16098. Vitamin D is 0.0 mcg: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #16098. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 mcg: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #16098 — peanut butter is a plant food with no B12. Trans fat is 0.0 g: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #16098 — natural peanut butter contains no hydrogenated oils. Water_g (1.8 g) estimated from USDA SR Legacy #16098 moisture data. Monounsaturated fat (24.5 g) and polyunsaturated fat (12.3 g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy #16098 fatty acid profile. PEANUT ALLERGY: peanut (Arachis hypogaea) is the most common cause of severe anaphylaxis in Singapore; always confirm allergy status before recommending. Aflatoxin: commercial brands in Singapore are regulated by SFA (<15 ppb). This record represents the generic natural peanut butter category (no added salt, no added sugar, no hydrogenated oil); commercial brands (Jif, Skippy, Adams) may differ in sodium and sugar content. FoodEx2 code A0PNB is used as the closest defensible direct match for natural peanut butter in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
